mechanism_narrative: A persistent uptrend in industrial production signals expanding output capacity and strong economic activity, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO trend vs forward 20-day returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the signal produces negative returns more than 45% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 4.5%, it indicates a tightening cycle that increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns after trigger are positive more than 40% of the time, or the average return difference between trigger and non-trigger periods is not statistically significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent low unemployment (below 5%) and high payroll employment (above 135,000) indicate a tight labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, fueling positive equity returns.
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions hold, forward 20-day returns are not positive on average over any 2-year window, or the joint signal does not improve upon individual series signals.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 5%), a month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions and economic recovery, leading to positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: In the high unemployment regime, months with falling UNRATE do not produce higher average 20-day returns than months with rising UNRATE, or the hit ratio is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate robust residential construction and consumer demand, which feeds into broader economic growth and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The correlation between HOUST changes and forward 60-day returns is not positive and statistically significant over rolling 5-year windows, or the top quartile of HOUST moves produces median returns less than zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the CPI index above 200 signals building inflationary pressures that force the Fed to tighten policy, compressing valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: After the CPI crosses 200, forward 5-day returns are not negative on average over a 2-year span, or the average return is within ±1 standard deviation of the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in both retail sales (above $350B) and industrial production (above 120) signals synchronized demand and supply expansion, reinforcing earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition does not produce positive average 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction adds no predictive power beyond the individual series (p > 0.05 in regression).

mechanism_narrative: In a moderate rate environment (FEDFUNDS between 2% and 5%), a rise in the federal funds rate reflects expected growth and is absorbed without harming valuations, leading to positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: In the moderate rate regime, months with rising FEDFUNDS do not have higher average 60-day returns than months with falling FEDFUNDS, or the hit ratio is below 50%.
